title: 牙医谋杀案
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\203-牙医谋杀案-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Mr. Barnes meets with Hercule Poirot and suggests that Ambrosio was merely a scapegoat who had been set up. Barnes warns that individuals will target Alistair Brent to earn millions of pounds, advising Poirot to watch Brent's associates, including his relatives, servants, a pharmacist, or a liquor merchant. Poirot also notes a mark on a carpet that suggests a body was dragged, leading him to conclude that Mortimer was killed in his office rather than his surgery, which implies the killer was someone inside the house.

The following morning, Poirot calls Inspector Japp to report that Miss Mabelle Sainsbury-Sil has disappeared from the Glengowell Court Hotel. She left the hotel around 7:00 pm without her luggage, which remained in her room. Japp notes that her background in India is clear and she is not involved in the murder case. The hotel manager, Mrs. Harrison, expresses concern regarding the disappearance.

Poirot and Japp inspect Miss Sainsbury-Sil's room, which is tidy and contains her suitcases and various shoes. Poirot observes that her black satin evening shoes are one size smaller than her other footwear. Japp finds letters and charity receipts in her vanity, noting she has no outstanding bills. Evidence suggests she intended to return for dinner, as she had not informed the restaurant of her absence and had previously told a staff member she would return to play cards. Poirot and Japp visit the Adams family in Hampstead, who knew Miss Sainsbury-Sil from India but had not seen her for a month. Mrs. Adams provides Poirot with the address of a hotel near Russell Square and other addresses of her friends in Streatham.
